paper_title: Molecular and Morphological Analysis of Snake Specimens

methods.description: This method involves extracting genomic DNA, amplifying specific genes, and performing phylogenetic analysis to elucidate the evolutionary relationships of snake species.

steps.description: Genomic DNA was extracted using Qiagen DNeasy TM Tissue kits.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA using Qiagen DNeasy Tissue kits

steps.description: Amplification of three mitochondrial (12s & ND4 for two specimens & 16s for only the one specimen) and one nuclear genes (c-mos for two specimens) following Pyron et al.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify mitochondrial and nuclear genes using PCR

steps.description: PCR reaction of 10μl were set containing 4μl Qiagen TM Taq PCR Master Mix, 3μl water, 0.5μl of each forward and reverse primer and 2μl of genomic DNA were set on a temperature gradient for temperature optimization using a Eppendorf Mastercycler Nexus GSX1.
steps.searchable_summary: set up PCR reaction with temperature gradient optimization

steps.description: PRC products were cleaned with QIAquick PCR Purification Kit and were sequenced with a 3730 DNA Analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: clean PCR products and sequence DNA

steps.description: Sequences of protein coding genes were aligned using MACSE to check for frameshifts and codons and were concatenated with non-coding gene sequences in Geneious R6 v.6.18.
Performed a Maximum Likelihood analysis of 410 species of snakes based on~570bp of cmos gene.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Maximum Likelihood analysis on snake species

steps.description: Concatenated data of five genes and performed a Maximum Likelihood analysis in RAxML v. 8.0.0 for 48 taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Maximum Likelihood analysis with RAxML

steps.description: Bayesian analysis was performed with BEAST v.1.7.3 with concatenated cmos and cytb sequences for Bayesian tree with posterior probability support.
The tree output file was visualized and edited in FigTree v1.3.1.
Ancestral area reconstruction was performed on Bayesian tree with RASP.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ancestral area reconstruction with RASP

methods.description: This method involves the morphological examination of snake specimens, including measurements, scale counts, and imaging, to provide detailed physical descriptions.

steps.description: Specimens were fixed in 4% formalin and later stored in 70% ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and store specimens in formalin and ethanol

steps.description: Measurements were taken with a Mitutoyo TM digital caliper to the nearest 0.01mm and total length was measured using a thread.
steps.searchable_summary: measure specimens with digital caliper and thread

steps.description: Ventral scales were counted following Dowling and scale reduction method follows Dowling with modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: count ventral scales and apply scale reduction method

steps.description: Maxillary and palatine bones were dissected from the holotype to study the number and nature of dentition.
Images were taken with the help of a Canon 70D mounted with a Canon 100mm macro and illuminated with the help of a two Canon 430EXII speedlight.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of specimens with Canon 70D and macro lens

steps.description: Sea level rise imagery were taken from http://calculatedearth.com/ to ascertain isolation of Saurashtra from mainland India.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch sea level rise imagery from calculatedearth.com

steps.description: These images were recreated in Adobe Photoshop CS6 and QGIS for publication.
steps.searchable_summary: recreate images in Adobe Photoshop and QGIS
